title: eSIM was supposed to replace SIM cards, but carriers turned it into a trap
excerpt: Virtually imperfect, but it could be better.
content_html:
Published Jul 20, 2026, 11:55 AM EDT

Sydney Butler is a technology writer with over 20 years of experience as a freelance PC technician and system builder and over a decade as a professional writer. He's worked for more than a decade in user education. On How-To Geek, he writes commerce content, guides, opinions, and specializes in editing hardware and cutting edge technology articles.

Sydney started working as a freelance computer technician around the age of 13, before which he was in charge of running the computer center for his school. (He also ran LAN gaming tournaments when the teachers weren't looking!) His interests include VR, PC, Mac, gaming, 3D printing, consumer electronics, the web, and privacy.

He holds a Master of Arts degree in Research Psychology with a minor in media and technology studies. His masters dissertation examined the potential for social media to spread misinformation.

Outside of How-To Geek, he hosts the Online Tech Tips YouTube Channel, and writes for Online Tech Tips, Switching to Mac, and Helpdesk Geek. Sydney also writes for Expert Reviews UK.

He also has bylines at 9to5Mac, 9to5Google, 9to5Toys, Tom's Hardware, MakeTechEasier, and Laptop Mag.

SIM cards are a relic of an age where multiple people had to share a single phone, and the cards themselves were enormous. These days, SIM cards are tiny and technically unnecessary thanks to the rise of eSIMs.

These virtual SIM cards were meant to make using a cellular connection about as easy as logging in to someone's Wi-Fi, while removing all the physical SIM card headaches we've grudgingly learned to live with. The thing is, for every problem eSIM technology promised to solve, it brought its own as a replacement.

eSIM promised frictionless switching, but carriers kept the friction

A new invisible prison

The most exciting promise of eSIM technology was that you'd be free of physical SIM cards linking your phone to a specific network. In the past, for example, when I traveled abroad, I had to buy a local SIM card at my destination airport rather than pay the extortionate roaming charges of my contract provider. I'd put my contract SIM in the hotel safe, or otherwise ensure that I wouldn't lose it. Then swapped back when I got home.

With an eSIM, that process is indeed much better. I can even sign up with an eSIM service like Saily, which offers seamless switching when you visit certain countries without the silly roaming costs.

Samsung Galaxy S26 Ultra

SoC
Snapdragon 8 Elite Gen 5

Display
6.9-inch Dynamic Super AMOLED 2X

Get the new Galaxy S26 Ultra with AI smarts and an all-new privacy display. It's big, powerful, packed with AI, and you'll love the S-Pen stylus. 

RAM
12 or 16 GB

Storage
256GB, 512GB, or 1TB

Battery
5,000 mAh

Operating System
Android

Front camera
12MP f/2.2

Rear camera
200MP f/1.4 main, 50MP f/1.9 ultrawide, 10MP f/2.4 telephoto, 50MP f/2.9 telephoto

Dimensions
163.6 x 78.1 x 7.9 mm (6.44 x 3.07 x 0.31 in)

Colors
Violet, Sky Blue, Black, White, Silver Shadow, Pink Gold

Weight
214g

Charge speed
60W wired Super Fast Charging, 25W wireless

Stylus
S-Pen

But, how little friction you have when setting up or switching between eSIMs is still entirely up to the provider in question. So, in many cases, you still have to jump through numerous hoops when activating and managing eSIMs. Even worse, if you have a carrier-locked phone eSIMs from other networks still won't work.

Moving your number between phones is now more complicated

It's a joint decision

I just switched from my old iPhone 14 Pro to a new Samsung Galaxy S25 Ultra, and, as usual, the process was as simple as popping the SIM out of the old phone and putting it into the new. My number is tied to that SIM card and as long as the SIM itself is functional, I can keep going without any help.

But with an eSIM if my old phone is broken or stolen, it complicates things significantly, and in many cases just a regular transfer from one phone to another can involve multiple steps that can even require a call to customer support. Of course, since eSIMs remove the security of a physical SIM it's necessary to have some friction in place to prevent virtual SIM-swap fraud. And, of course, physical SIM swap scams happen too.

Maybe it's not a huge deal if you're only changing phones every few years, but for a certain group of people this extra friction is a good reason to avoid eSIMs.

Apple iPhone 17 Pro Max

SoC
A19 Pro chip

Display
6.9-inches

The iPhone 17 Pro Max has many of the same specs as the iPhone 17 Pro, but you get a larger screen and up to 2TB of storage.

Storage
256GB, 526GB, 1TB, 2TB

Battery
Up to 39 hours of video playback

Ports
USB-C

Operating System
iOS

Colors
Silver, Cosmic Orange, Deep Blue

Display type
Super Retina XDR display

The idea is still good, but the ecosystem isn’t ready

Caught in the SIM paradigm

In the end, the idea of eSIMs is a good one, but in practice it's not yet ready to fulfill the promise of a fully digital cellular access system. At the very least, we need some sort of universal standard for what the sign-up and transfer process is for eSIMs.

Ideally, there should be no reliance on a third party to stand in your way, and a device-to-device eSIM feature should be standard in my opinion. In particular, moving eSIMs from iPhone to Android and vice versa needs to be a simpler process.

Carrier restrictions that have come over from physical SIMs to eSIMs need to be rethought. Switching between eSIMs on the same device could do with some more refinement, if you ask me.

eSIMs are already an outdated concept

To be honest, what I'm really hoping for is a system that isn't just a digital copy of how physical SIM cards operate. I want a future where you can switch on a phone, scan for available carriers and just sign up with a username and password. Just like Wi-Fi in a coffee shop. We've moved beyond needing SIM cards and phone numbers as factors for authentication. With technologies like passkeys now a reality, there has to be a better way to handle mobile subscriptions.

sentences.zh: 但使用 eSIM 时，如果我的旧手机损坏或被盗，事情就会变得非常复杂，在许多情况下，只是从一部手机到另一部手机的定期传输就可能涉及多个步骤，甚至需要致电客户支持。

sentences.en: Of course, since eSIMs remove the security of a physical SIM it's necessary to have some friction in place to prevent virtual SIM-swap fraud.
sentences.zh: 当然，由于 eSIM 消除了物理 SIM 卡的安全性，因此有必要采取一些措施来防止虚拟 SIM 卡交换欺诈。

sentences.en: And, of course, physical SIM swap scams happen too.
sentences.zh: 当然，物理 SIM 卡交换诈骗也会发生。

blocks.html_en: Maybe it's not a huge deal if you're only changing phones every few years, but for a certain group of people this extra friction is a good reason to avoid eSIMs.

sentences.en: Maybe it's not a huge deal if you're only changing phones every few years, but for a certain group of people this extra friction is a good reason to avoid eSIMs.
sentences.zh: 如果您每隔几年才更换一次手机，也许这并不是什么大问题，但对于某些人群来说，这种额外的摩擦是避免使用 eSIM 的一个很好的理由。

blocks.html_en: The idea is still good, but the ecosystem isn’t ready

sentences.en: The idea is still good, but the ecosystem isn’t ready
sentences.zh: 这个想法仍然不错，但生态系统还没有准备好

blocks.html_en: Caught in the SIM paradigm

sentences.en: Caught in the SIM paradigm
sentences.zh: 陷入 SIM 范式

blocks.html_en: In the end, the idea of eSIMs is a good one, but in practice it's not yet ready to fulfill the promise of a fully digital cellular access system. At the very least, we need some sort of universal standard for what the sign-up and transfer process is for eSIMs.

sentences.en: In the end, the idea of eSIMs is a good one, but in practice it's not yet ready to fulfill the promise of a fully digital cellular access system.
sentences.zh: 最后，eSIM 的想法是一个很好的想法，但实际上它尚未准备好履行全数字蜂窝接入系统的承诺。

sentences.en: At the very least, we need some sort of universal standard for what the sign-up and transfer process is for eSIMs.
sentences.zh: 至少，我们需要某种通用标准来规定 eSIM 的注册和转移流程。

blocks.html_en: Ideally, there should be no reliance on a third party to stand in your way, and a device-to-device eSIM feature should be standard in my opinion. In particular, moving eSIMs from iPhone to Android and vice versa needs to be a simpler process.

sentences.en: Ideally, there should be no reliance on a third party to stand in your way, and a device-to-device eSIM feature should be standard in my opinion.
sentences.zh: 理想情况下，不应该依赖第三方来阻碍您，并且我认为设备到设备的 eSIM 功能应该成为标准。

sentences.en: In particular, moving eSIMs from iPhone to Android and vice versa needs to be a simpler process.
sentences.zh: 特别是，将 eSIM 从 iPhone 转移到 Android 以及反之亦然需要一个更简单的过程。

blocks.html_en: Carrier restrictions that have come over from physical SIMs to eSIMs need to be rethought. Switching between eSIMs on the same device could do with some more refinement, if you ask me.

sentences.en: Carrier restrictions that have come over from physical SIMs to eSIMs need to be rethought.
sentences.zh: 需要重新考虑从物理 SIM 卡到 eSIM 卡的运营商限制。

sentences.en: Switching between eSIMs on the same device could do with some more refinement, if you ask me.
sentences.zh: 如果你问我的话，在同一设备上的 eSIM 之间切换可能需要更多改进。

blocks.html_en: eSIMs are already an outdated concept

sentences.en: eSIMs are already an outdated concept
sentences.zh: eSIM 已经是一个过时的概念

blocks.html_en: To be honest, what I'm really hoping for is a system that isn't just a digital copy of how physical SIM cards operate. I want a future where you can switch on a phone, scan for available carriers and just sign up with a username and password. Just like Wi-Fi in a coffee shop. We've moved beyond needing SIM cards and phone numbers as factors for authentication. With technologies like passkeys now a reality, there has to be a better way to handle mobile subscriptions.

sentences.en: To be honest, what I'm really hoping for is a system that isn't just a digital copy of how physical SIM cards operate.
sentences.zh: 说实话，我真正希望的是一个不仅仅是物理 SIM 卡操作方式的数字副本的系统。

sentences.en: I want a future where you can switch on a phone, scan for available carriers and just sign up with a username and password.
sentences.zh: 我希望未来你可以打开手机，扫描可用的运营商，然后只需使用用户名和密码即可注册。

sentences.en: Just like Wi-Fi in a coffee shop.
sentences.zh: 就像咖啡店里的 Wi-Fi 一样。

sentences.en: We've moved beyond needing SIM cards and phone numbers as factors for authentication.
sentences.zh: 我们不再需要 SIM 卡和电话号码作为身份验证因素。

sentences.en: With technologies like passkeys now a reality, there has to be a better way to handle mobile subscriptions.
sentences.zh: 随着万能钥匙等技术现已成为现实，必须有更好的方法来处理移动订阅。

content_html_zh:
发布于 2026 年 7 月 20 日上午 11:55（美国东部时间）

Sydney Butler 是一位技术作家，拥有 20 多年的自由 PC 技术人员和系统构建人员经验以及十多年的专业作家经验。 他在用户教育领域工作了十多年。 他在 How-To Geek 上撰写商业内容、指南、观点，并专门编辑硬件和尖端技术文章。

Sydney 13 岁左右开始担任自由计算机技术员，在此之前他负责运营学校的计算机中心。 （他还趁老师不注意的时候举办局域网游戏锦标赛！） 他的兴趣包括 VR、PC、Mac、游戏、3D 打印、消费电子产品、网络和隐私。

他拥有研究心理学文学硕士学位，辅修媒体和技术研究。 他的硕士论文研究了社交媒体传播错误信息的潜力。

除了 How-To Geek 之外，他还主持 Online Tech Tips YouTube 频道，并为 Online Tech Tips、Switching to Mac 和 Helpdesk Geek 撰稿。 悉尼还为英国专家评论撰稿。

他还在 9to5Mac、9to5Google、9to5Toys、Tom's Hardware、MakeTechEasier 和 Laptop Mag 上发表署名。

SIM 卡是多人共用一部手机的时代的遗物，而且卡本身也很大。 如今，由于 eSIM 的兴起，SIM 卡体积很小，从技术上讲也没有必要。

这些虚拟 SIM 卡的目的是使使用蜂窝连接就像登录某人的 Wi-Fi 一样简单，同时消除我们勉强忍受的所有物理 SIM 卡问题。 事实是，对于 eSIM 技术承诺解决的每个问题，它都会带来自己的替代品。

eSIM 承诺无摩擦切换，但运营商却保留了摩擦

新的隐形监狱

eSIM 技术最令人兴奋的承诺是，您将不再需要将手机连接到特定网络的物理 SIM 卡。 例如，过去，当我出国旅行时，我必须在目的地机场购买当地的SIM卡，而不是向签约提供商支付高昂的漫游费。 我会把合同 SIM 卡放在酒店的保险箱里，或者以其他方式确保我不会丢失它。 然后回家就换回来了。

有了 eSIM，这个过程确实要好得多。 我什至可以注册像 Saily 这样的 eSIM 服务，当您访问某些国家/地区时，该服务可以提供无缝切换，而无需支付愚蠢的漫游费用。

三星 Galaxy S26 Ultra

SoC Snapdragon 8 Elite Gen 5

显示屏 6.9 英寸动态 Super AMOLED 2X

获取配备 AI 智能和全新隐私显示屏的新款 Galaxy S26 Ultra。 它体积大、功能强大、充满人工智能，而且您一定会喜欢 S-Pen 手写笔。

内存 12 或 16 GB

存储 256GB、512GB 或 1TB

电池 5,000 毫安时

操作系统 安卓

前置摄像头 12MP f/2.2

后置摄像头 200MP f/1.4 主摄、50MP f/1.9 超广角、10MP f/2.4 长焦、50MP f/2.9 长焦

尺寸 163.6 x 78.1 x 7.9 毫米（6.44 x 3.07 x 0.31 英寸）

颜色 紫罗兰色、天蓝色、黑色、白色、银影、粉金

重量 214克

充电速度 60W 有线超级快充，25W 无线

手写笔 S 笔

但是，设置或在 eSIM 之间切换时遇到的麻烦有多大仍然完全取决于相关提供商。 因此，在许多情况下，您在激活和管理 eSIM 时仍然需要克服重重困难。 更糟糕的是，如果您拥有运营商锁定的手机，来自其他网络的 eSIM 仍然无法使用。

在手机之间转移号码现在变得更加复杂

这是一个共同的决定

我刚刚从旧的 iPhone 14 Pro 换到了新的三星 Galaxy S25 Ultra，和往常一样，整个过程就像从旧手机中取出 SIM 卡并将其放入新手机一样简单。 我的号码与该 SIM 卡绑定，只要 SIM 卡本身可以正常工作，我就可以继续使用而无需任何帮助。

但使用 eSIM 时，如果我的旧手机损坏或被盗，事情就会变得非常复杂，在许多情况下，只是从一部手机到另一部手机的定期传输就可能涉及多个步骤，甚至需要致电客户支持。 当然，由于 eSIM 消除了物理 SIM 卡的安全性，因此有必要采取一些措施来防止虚拟 SIM 卡交换欺诈。 当然，物理 SIM 卡交换诈骗也会发生。

如果您每隔几年才更换一次手机，也许这并不是什么大问题，但对于某些人群来说，这种额外的摩擦是避免使用 eSIM 的一个很好的理由。

苹果 iPhone 17 Pro Max

SoC A19 Pro芯片

显示屏 6.9英寸

iPhone 17 Pro Max 的许多规格与 iPhone 17 Pro 相同，但屏幕更大，存储容量高达 2TB。

存储 256GB、526GB、1TB、2TB

电池 视频播放时间长达 39 小时

端口 USB-C

操作系统 iOS

颜色 银色、宇宙橙、深蓝色

显示屏类型 超视网膜 XDR 显示屏

这个想法仍然不错，但生态系统还没有准备好

陷入 SIM 范式

最后，eSIM 的想法是一个很好的想法，但实际上它尚未准备好履行全数字蜂窝接入系统的承诺。 至少，我们需要某种通用标准来规定 eSIM 的注册和转移流程。

理想情况下，不应该依赖第三方来阻碍您，并且我认为设备到设备的 eSIM 功能应该成为标准。 特别是，将 eSIM 从 iPhone 转移到 Android 以及反之亦然需要一个更简单的过程。

需要重新考虑从物理 SIM 卡到 eSIM 卡的运营商限制。 如果你问我的话，在同一设备上的 eSIM 之间切换可能需要更多改进。

eSIM 已经是一个过时的概念

说实话，我真正希望的是一个不仅仅是物理 SIM 卡操作方式的数字副本的系统。 我希望未来你可以打开手机，扫描可用的运营商，然后只需使用用户名和密码即可注册。 就像咖啡店里的 Wi-Fi 一样。 我们不再需要 SIM 卡和电话号码作为身份验证因素。 随着万能钥匙等技术现已成为现实，必须有更好的方法来处理移动订阅。
